official_notes: Old report baseline only; target side had no revision priority.
Old report assessment: official reviewer passed the resume as substantively strong.
claude_notes: ATS-like insistence that Looker and semantic-layer signals appear as exact phrases in body text.
Exact year arithmetic concerns around 3+ years claims.
Summary framing judged too data-modeling-first rather than infrastructure/performance-first.

metadata.label: same/Google
metadata.title: Software Engineer III, Full Stack, Google Cloud Platforms
metadata.team_direction: Google is a leading technology company that develops next-generation technologies impacting billions of users

official_notes: Old report baseline only; target side had no revision priority.
Old report assessment: official reviewer passed the resume as substantively strong.
claude_notes: ATS-like insistence that Looker and semantic-layer signals appear as exact phrases in body text.
Exact year arithmetic concerns around 3+ years claims.
Summary framing judged too data-modeling-first rather than infrastructure/performance-first.

findings.field: Experience bullets across TikTok / DiDi / project sections
findings.issue: Key scope and scale signals are not consistently emphasized, even though the resume already bolds many technical terms. Examples include `13-person`, `20+ city`, `12K+`, `3 active markets`, and `11 operational releases`.
findings.fix: Bold only the highest-value scope/result tokens in the top bullets so the eye lands on scale faster, while keeping the emphasis selective.

findings.issue: Qualifier words are being bolded in the body, such as `team-maintained`, which weakens the visual convention that bold should reserve space for technologies and scope signals.
findings.fix: Unbold qualifier/adjective phrases like `team-maintained` and keep bolding for technologies, scale, and outcome numbers.

findings.issue: The first sentence leads with a transition narrative (`transitioning from data analysis to full-stack development`) instead of a direct role/domain anchor, which weakens the first-screen signal.
findings.fix: Rewrite the first sentence to start with a direct role framing, such as a full-stack or backend/security-oriented anchor, and move the transition story later or out of the summary.

findings.issue: The first bullet is technically solid but still reads as a metric-led improvement bullet rather than the clearest scope/ownership signal for the role.
findings.fix: Move the strongest ownership or scope cue to the front of the bullet, then keep the latency metric as the payoff.

findings.issue: The body uses `AWS Bedrock`, but `Bedrock` does not appear in Skills, so the named platform is unsupported in the skill inventory.
findings.fix: Add `AWS Bedrock` to Skills if this project bullet stays, so the named platform has matching skill coverage.

revision_priority: [MUST] Rewrite Summary sentence 1 to lead with a direct role/domain anchor instead of `transitioning from data analysis to full-stack development`.
[MUST] Add `AWS Bedrock` to Skills, or remove the named platform from the TikTok retrieval project bullet.
[SHOULD] Move the clearest scope signal into the first TikTok bullet and surface the best quantitative evidence earlier.
[NICE] Normalize bolding so qualifiers like `team-maintained` are unbolded and only technologies or key scale/result tokens are emphasized.
raw_json.revision_instructions: Keep all immutable employer/title/date/location fields unchanged. Rewrite Summary sentence 1 to start with a direct role anchor, e.g. a full-stack or security-focused SWE framing, and avoid leading with the transition narrative. Add `AWS Bedrock` to Skills if the TikTok retrieval project remains as written. In the first TikTok bullet, put the ownership/scope cue before the metric, and bold only the most important scale/result tokens. Unbold qualifier words such as `team-maintained` so bolding is reserved for technologies and quantified impact.
